title: Architecture Evaluation, Quality Attributes, and Decision Governance · Decision Evidence Lens: Architecture Viewpoints
summary: The capstone architecture diagram shows components but not quality scenarios, trust boundaries, failure containment, evolution, cost, or rejected alternatives. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the capstone architecture viewpoints increment in evaluated architecture and decision governance packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: Describe context, containers, components, data, deployment, runtime, trust, operations, and ownership views at useful levels.
Track accepted evidence, changed assumptions, blocked claims, forecast, decision latency, quality debt, and residual risk rather than percent complete.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Architecture Viewpoints · Theory Architecture Viewpoints
steps.instruction:
theory case — A box diagram omits quality tradeoffs, trust and failure boundaries, cost, evolution, dissent, review triggers, and rejected alternatives. The theory variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Architecture Viewpoints contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Architecture Viewpoints · Theory Architecture Viewpoints.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for describe context, containers, components, data, deployment, runtime, trust, operations, and ownership views at useful levels. For Predict Architecture Viewpoints · Theory Architecture Viewpoints, keep Architecture Viewpoints connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Describe context, containers, components, data, deployment, runtime, trust, operations, and ownership views at useful levels. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Architecture Viewpoints while producing evaluated architecture and decision governance packet.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A box diagram omits quality tradeoffs, trust and failure boundaries, cost, evolution, dissent, review triggers, and rejected alternatives. The worked example variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Describe context, containers, components, data, deployment, runtime, trust, operations, and ownership views at useful levels. A defensible solution applies Architecture Viewpoints, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to evaluated architecture and decision governance packet.

content.code:
# Worked focus: capstone-architecture-viewpoints — Describe context, containers, components, data, deployment, runtime, trust, operations, and ownership views at useful levels.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-2
focus: capstone-architecture-viewpoints
decision-evidence: Describe context, containers, components, data, deployment, runtime, trust, operations, and ownership views at useful levels.
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
